text:
The first untethered space walk was a significant milestone in space exploration, demonstrating the capability of astronauts to maneuver freely in space without a safety tether. This was achieved using the Manned Maneuvering Unit (MMU).

The first astronaut to perform an untethered space walk was Bruce McCandless II. This historic event took place on February 7, 1984, during the STS-41-B mission. The mission was conducted using the Space Shuttle Challenger. McCandless exited the Challenger, propelled by the MMU, and floated freely in space, a feat previously only imagined.

FINAL ANSWER: The first untethered space walk took place from the Space Shuttle Challenger.
